summary: Make float printers consult *read-default-float-format* so the exponent marker is emitted only when a float's format differs from the current default.

items.title: Float printers honor *read-default-float-format*
items.description:
Fix print_double_float and print_single_float in src/core/printer.c so each emits an exponent marker only when the float's format differs from *read-default-float-format* (CLHS 22.1.3.1.3).

Step A — expose the symbol to C: add a new global `CL_Obj SYM_READ_DEFAULT_FLOAT_FORMAT` following the EXACT pattern of the existing SYM_PRINT_* globals (verified to survive the moving GC via the package-registry root chain — no extra cl_gc_register_root call needed). (1) In src/core/symbol.h, near the printer-control symbol externs (~line 233), add `extern CL_Obj SYM_READ_DEFAULT_FLOAT_FORMAT;`. (2) In src/core/symbol.c, near the other SYM_ global definitions (~line 198), add `CL_Obj SYM_READ_DEFAULT_FLOAT_FORMAT = CL_NIL;`. (3) In src/core/symbol.c at the existing *READ-DEFAULT-FLOAT-FORMAT* init block (lines 724-730), assign the global instead of a throwaway local: set `SYM_READ_DEFAULT_FLOAT_FORMAT = cl_intern_in("*READ-DEFAULT-FLOAT-FORMAT*", 27, cl_package_cl);`, then mark CL_SYM_SPECIAL and set its value to `cl_intern("SINGLE-FLOAT", 12)` exactly as now.

Step B — add a helper in src/core/printer.c (near the existing print_*_p() readers / before print_single_float at ~line 504): a static `int default_float_is_double(void)` that returns 1 when the default designates a double. Implementation: if `CL_NULL_P(SYM_READ_DEFAULT_FLOAT_FORMAT)` return 0 (before init); read `val = cl_symbol_value(SYM_READ_DEFAULT_FLOAT_FORMAT)`; if `!CL_HEAP_P(val)` return 0; if `CL_HDR_TYPE(CL_OBJ_TO_PTR(val)) != TYPE_SYMBOL` return 0; get `name = cl_symbol_name(val)`; return `(strcmp(name, "DOUBLE-FLOAT") == 0 || strcmp(name, "LONG-FLOAT") == 0)`. (CL_HDR_TYPE, TYPE_SYMBOL, CL_HEAP_P, cl_symbol_name and <string.h> are already used/included in printer.c.)

Step C — rewrite print_double_float (lines 525-541): compute `int omit_marker = default_float_is_double();`. After `sprintf(buf, "%.15g", value);` locate an exponent char (`e = strchr(buf,'e'); if(!e) e = strchr(buf,'E');`). If found (scientific): set `*e = omit_marker ? 'e' : 'd'`. Else if `needs_decimal(buf)`: `strcat(buf, omit_marker ? ".0" : ".0d0")`. Else if `!omit_marker`: `strcat(buf, "d0")` (when omit_marker and the buffer already contains a '.', leave it markerless). Then out_str(buf). Net effect: default single-float -> doubles still print with d0 (unchanged); default double-float -> doubles print markerless (the reported bug).

Step D — rewrite print_single_float (lines 516-523): compute `int emit_marker = default_float_is_double();` (single's format differs from default exactly when the default is double). After `sprintf(buf, "%g", (double)value);` locate exponent char as above. If found (scientific): set `*e = emit_marker ? 'f' : 'e'`. Else if `needs_decimal(buf)`: `strcat(buf, emit_marker ? ".0f0" : ".0")`. Else if `emit_marker`: `strcat(buf, "f0")`. Then out_str(buf). Net effect: default single-float -> singles still markerless (unchanged); default double-float -> singles print with f0 (the latent mirror defect).

Buffer sizes (single buf[32], double buf[48]) are sufficient for the added 2-4 chars; keep them.

Step E — regression tests (host): in tests/test_float.c add TEST functions using the existing eval_print helper and register them with RUN(...) in the runner at the bottom. Note eval_print prin1's the eval result, so do the float printing INSIDE the dynamic binding via prin1-to-string and expect the surrounding quotes. Cases:
  - default state double w/ marker: eval_print("(prin1-to-string 0.0146878d0)") == "\"0.0146877998486161d0\"" (unchanged behavior).
  - bug fix: eval_print("(let ((*read-default-float-format* 'double-float)) (prin1-to-string 0.0146878d0))") == "\"0.0146877998486161\"".
  - double markerless for whole value: eval_print("(let ((*read-default-float-format* 'double-float)) (prin1-to-string 5.0d0))") == "\"5.0\"".
  - single default-single markerless: eval_print("(prin1-to-string 1.5)") == "\"1.5\"".
  - single under double default gets f0: eval_print("(let ((*read-default-float-format* 'double-float)) (prin1-to-string 1.5))") == "\"1.5f0\"".
  - single whole value under double default: eval_print("(let ((*read-default-float-format* 'double-float)) (prin1-to-string 5.0))") == "\"5.0f0\"".

Step F — regression tests (Amiga): in tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp, in the float-printing area (near the prin1-to-string float checks / the float-basics section around lines 1581-1606 or the prin1-to-string section ~1933), add equivalent (check "..." <expected-string> <form>) lines mirroring the host cases above, e.g. (check "print double default-single" "0.0146877998486161d0" (prin1-to-string 0.0146878d0)), (check "print double default-double markerless" "0.0146877998486161" (let ((*read-default-float-format* 'double-float)) (prin1-to-string 0.0146878d0))), (check "print single default-double f0" "1.5f0" (let ((*read-default-float-format* 'double-float)) (prin1-to-string 1.5))), plus the 5.0/5.0d0/markerless variants.

Run `make test` (and ideally `make -f Makefile.cross test-amiga`) — all must pass.
